content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 607 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance/Administration    •   •   •    Executive/Legal          Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                                      322-5901                    322-6141

March 8, 1976

Kenneth C. Scheidig, City Attorney
Civic Center
200 Bernal Avenue
P.O. Drawer C
Pleasanton, CA. 94566

Re: Request for Opinion
No. 76-029

Dear Mr. Scheidzig:

Thank you for your letter of February 18, 1976, requesting an opinion concerning anonymous contributions. Because you have indicated the need for immediate advice, the Commission will not issue a formal opinion in response to your request. However, I hope the following informal comments will provide some guidance to you.

It is the opinion of the Commission that Government Code Section 84304 prohibits a candidate from receiving $50 or more in anonymous contributions from the same source. Although, under the hypothetical facts you posit, a candidate may be receiving more than $50 from the same source and not know it for reporting purposes, the interpretation you urge is overly restrictive and may seriously impede the ability of candidates to "pass the hat" or engage in other fundraising activities to gather small contributions from unidentified persons. It is the view of the Commission that only in those situations where the candidate knows or has reason to know a contributor is circumventing the law by giving a series of anonymous contributions would the candidate be violating Government Code Section 84304.

If you have any further questions, please contact Natalie West, an attorney with the Legal Division of the Commission staff.

Sincerely,

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman,
For the Commission

DHL:pvp

The image appears to be almost entirely blank or white, with only a few very faint marks visible at the top and right edge that are too unclear to transcribe with confidence.

CITY OF PLEASANTON
OFFICE OF CITY ATTORNEY- Received 2-24-76

KENNETH C. SCHEIDIG
CITY ATTORNEY

CIVIC CENTER
200 BERNAL AVENUE
P. O. DRAWER C
PLEASANTON, CALIFORNIA 94566
TELEPHONE (415) 846-3202

5-9-96 HARVEY E. LEVINE
DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY

7630/

February 18, 1976


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100"K" Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Commissioners:

The City Attorney's office of the City of Pleasanton requests
an opinion concerning Government Code Section 84304. As you
know, that section prohibits the making of anonymous contributions
to a candidate or committee or any other person totaling $50.00
or more in a calendar year. In addition, it requires the candidate
to promptly pay to the Secretary of State for the deposit in the
general fund, any anonymous contributions of $50.00 or more.

Should this section be interpreted to mean that a candidate may
receive numerous contributions from anonymous donors of $49.99
or less? Or should Proposition 9 be interpreted to require Section
84304 to be read in conjunction with the reporting section, Gov-
ernmental Code Section 84210(g), to the effect that no candidate
shall retain for his use in his campaign more than $49.99 in
anonymous contributions in a calendar year. The later reading
would insure that the candidate does not violate Section 84210(g),
which requires the reporting of the name and address of each person
contributing $50.00 or more.

The difficulty with the anonymous contribution section is that
a candidate may, in fact, be receiving more than $50.00 from a
single source and not know it for reporting purposes. This office
assumes that if a candidate could not show that the anonymous
contributions came from more than one source and each was less
than $50.00, the excess over $50.00 would have to be paid to the
Secretary of State.

As the March 2 election is nearly upon us, it may be impossible
for your Commission to render an opinion before the coming election.
However, this office would appreciate some guidance for future
elections on this matter.

Sincerely yours,

City Attorney
Pleasanton, California

KCS/sc
cc: City Manager
